steps.title: Identify how Spanish connects the languages
steps.content:
Both examples follow the same pattern: Indigenous language → Spanish → English. Cocoa came to English “from cacao, the Spanish word,” which Spanish had borrowed from Nahuatl. Iguana “entered English through Spanish” but originally came from Taíno.

Spanish is positioned as the intermediary—the bridge connecting Indigenous languages to English.

steps.title: Match Spanish's role to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A says Spanish “served as a medium through which Indigenous languages have influenced English.” That's exactly what the examples demonstrate: Spanish functioned as a conduit, allowing Indigenous words to reach English indirectly.

Choices B, C, and
  • D all overreach—B invents a quantity comparison never made
  • C suggests a two-way exchange when only the incoming direction appears
  • D overgeneralizes from two examples to claim equal numbers
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
